The main claim is: The problem is that the agent will probably predict that this text is not caused by aliens, but by the program it is running on.

top_comment.author: Joseph Bloom
top_comment.text:
"In this post, I'll present a way to turn LLMs into agents such that we can approximately model them as a utility maximizer."

If this works it would be very dangerous and kind of thing we would want to avoid. We're very lucky current systems are as poorly agentic as they are.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: "In this post, I'll present a way to turn LLMs into agents such that we can approximately model them as a utility maximizer." If this works it would be very dangerous and kind of thing we would want to avoid.

summary: The main claim is: That human level AIs are arriving seems much less controversial and they are themselves dangerous by virtue of the reasoning/scenario laid out above.
The main claim is: And thus it may be the case that for further improvements OOM more new data will need to be generated.
Not only this, but so far newer generations of AI have required more data than previous generations.

The main claim is: -- So even an AI with "human-level" intelligence will want to get more computing power, have safeguards against being shut down, and obtain resources like money and contacts.
A normal human is more concerned with comfort, more afraid of hard work, and more complacent to do what everyone else is doing: not much.
